name: Betawi
notes: Betawi (Betawi Malay / basa Betawi) — Malayic (Austronesian) creole-influenced language of Jakarta, Indonesia. Written in the Latin script following Indonesian orthographic conventions. GRAPHEME INVENTORY NOT YET SPECIFIED: Betawi has no codified standard orthography of its own and its phonology is not documented to a level that supports a reliable letter→phoneme table here. INPUT CONTRACT: input is expected in the Latin script, spelled per Indonesian conventions. wikidata_qid left unset: the code's Wikidata row resolves to the Wikimedia project item, not the language item; not recorded rather than record a wrong id.
